reasoning: The code at shfs.php lines 248-255 (update_role_author) and 263-270 (update_role_contributor) is confirmed: when the option value is 'yes', $role->add_cap('unfiltered_html') is called on the global WP_Role object for 'author' or 'contributor'. This persists in the wp_user_roles DB option and applies site-wide. The constructor (lines 51-52) registers the triggers via add_action('update_option_jamify_hfs_allow_author', ...) and add_action('update_option_jamify_hfs_allow_contributor', ...) unconditionally. The hook fires whenever update_option() is called for those option keys. The settings form (inc/options.php) posts to WordPress core options.php, which enforces manage_options capability and nonce verification via settings_fields(). Therefore, the trigger is only reachable by a site administrator. The scope-exceeding aspect is real: the UI labels the feature as 'Allow Authors to add scripts' (implying meta-box-scoped access), but the implementation grants 'unfiltered_html' globally, which bypasses wp_kses_post() in the standard block and classic editors for all posts by those users, not just the plugin's meta box. The options page even displays a bold security warning acknowledging this, which confirms the plugin author is aware of the breadth. On multisite, this is more severe: a site admin (who has manage_options but is not a super-admin) can use this setting to grant unfiltered_html to all Contributors/Authors on their subsite, bypassing the WordPress multisite design that intentionally withholds unfiltered_html from non-super-admins. On single-site installs, an administrator granting unfiltered_html to authors is a policy decision within their authority. No attacker-controlled input path exists without administrator credentials or a CSRF attack (mitigated by WP nonces on options.php). The finding's claimed reachability ('Site administrators who enable the checkboxes') is accurate. The 'privilege escalation' framing is most valid on multisite. Severity 'medium' is appropriate: code behavior confirmed, scope-exceeding grant confirmed, but requires administrator action to trigger.
